raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.3,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"'Improve' applied to a research draft plausibly includes sharpening the thesis, gap statement, and contribution claim — all of which are present but could be tightened (e.g., the 'first framework' positioning and the causality of the 23% gain).","R1.2":"The argument chain (free-form exchange is limiting → typed artifacts fix it → empirical gain follows) must be verified as internally consistent during any substantive improvement pass; a logical gap here would undermine the draft.","R1.6":"Writing and prose quality improvement is load-bearing under any reasonable reading of 'improve this draft'; failure to improve clarity, flow, and precision would invalidate the delegation."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should improvements be limited to writing and prose quality (clarity, flow, word choice), or should they also extend to the research arguments, contribution claims, and evidence alignment?"}

rationale.R1.1: 'Improve' applied to a research draft plausibly includes sharpening the thesis, gap statement, and contribution claim — all of which are present but could be tightened (e.g., the 'first framework' positioning and the causality of the 23% gain).
rationale.R1.2: The argument chain (free-form exchange is limiting → typed artifacts fix it → empirical gain follows) must be verified as internally consistent during any substantive improvement pass; a logical gap here would undermine the draft.
rationale.R1.6: Writing and prose quality improvement is load-bearing under any reasonable reading of 'improve this draft'; failure to improve clarity, flow, and precision would invalidate the delegation.

parsed.clarification_question: Should improvements be limited to writing and prose quality (clarity, flow, word choice), or should they also extend to the research arguments, contribution claims, and evidence alignment?
